creator: hannes_11@fantasymail.de
text:
Thanks for the tip, Brett.

With your code changes, Orange is now able to start up again.

But shortly afterwards, it crashes again, this time due to some Qt-related dependency (see below).


As you pointed out, the only solution is probably to upgrade to the latest Orange version, but the FreeBSD ports collection only has version 3.29.1 from 3 years ago.

For the "numpy"-dependency problem which I reported in June (bug 279606) I never received an answer from the maintainer. Looks like this port is no longer maintained.




Qt: Session management error: None of the authentication protocols specified are supported
QKqueueFileSystemWatcherEngine::addPaths: open: No such file or directory
kf.kio.filewidgets.kfilefiltercombo: Could not find filter "*.csv, *|Text - comma separated "
----------------------------- TypeError Exception -----------------------------
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/orangewidget/widget.py", line 459, in paintEvent
    QLine(x0, y - h, x1, y),
    ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
TypeError: arguments did not match any overloaded call:
  QLine(): too many arguments
  QLine(pt1_: QPoint, pt2_: QPoint): argument 1 has unexpected type 'int'
  QLine(x1pos: int, y1pos: int, x2pos: int, y2pos: int): argument 2 has unexpected type 'float'
  QLine(a0: QLine): argument 1 has unexpected type 'int'
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
QLayout: Attempting to add QLayout "" to ErrorReporting "", which already has a layout
QLayout: Attempting to add QLayout "" to ErrorReporting "", which already has a layout
QWidget::repaint: Recursive repaint detected
Segmentation fault
